title: Hieracium holopleuroides
taxonomy: Hieracium holopleuroides is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe of the sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Hieracium, which is commonly known as hawkweeds. The genus Hieracium is placed in the tribe Cichorieae, a group of dandelion-like plants within the sunflower family Asteraceae. The tribe Cichorieae is further classified within the subfamily Cichorioideae, which includes plants that have similar characteristics such as dandelions and hawkweeds. The exact classification of Hieracium holopleuroides may be subject to change based on ongoing research and revisions in plant taxonomy.
constituents: Hieracium holopleuroides contains sesquiterpene lactones, which are a class of compounds known for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These lactones may also have antioxidant and cytotoxic effects. However, the exact composition and specific active constituents of Hieracium holopleuroides are not well-documented, and further research is needed to fully understand its chemistry and potential uses. The plant's sesquiterpene lactones may be responsible for its reported medicinal properties, but more study is required to confirm their efficacy and safety. Further investigation into the plant's active constituents could lead to new discoveries and potential applications.

intro: Hieracium holopleuroides, commonly known as hawkweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, Hieracium holopleuroides has been used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and reduce the risk of infection. The plant's bioactive constituents, including sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Hieracium holopleuroides is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve to take advantage of its therapeutic benefits.
